summary: Adult families are DHS's smallest shelter population (well under 2,000 households on an average day), so this return-to-shelter rate is calculated on a small base and can swing several tenths of a point from a handful of cases in either direction. DHS reporting ties the FY22-FY25 decline to expanded subsidized housing placements, while the rise in the FY26 year-to-date figure is consistent with the small underlying case counts rather than a clear change in program performance.

sources.label: DHS, Preliminary Mayor's Management Report, Fiscal 2026
